summary: Iteration 2 — tested new auth/checkout/reviews/account/abandoned-cart features. Backend: 26/26 pytest pass (1 real bug found in brute-force lockout — request.client.host is k8s pod IP, identifier never accumulates). Frontend: register/login/logout/account flows, header User icon routing, /checkout address form, drawer Checkout → /checkout (not direct Stripe), Reviews section on ProductDetail, $6.99 shipping shown — all verified. Stripe redirect from /checkout proceed-payment-btn was not re-verified end-to-end this run (already passed in iter1) but the checkout flow up to that button works.

minor.issue: Lockout never engages behind k8s ingress. `request.client.host` returns the upstream pod IP (e.g. 10.227.1.2, 10.227.1.6) which varies per request, so the {ip:email} identifier never reaches count >= 5 in login_attempts collection. Verified by direct curl: 7 failed attempts → 7×401, login_attempts had two separate identifier rows with counts 5 and 2.
minor.fix: Read X-Forwarded-For header (first IP) or use only the email portion of the identifier for lockout. Recommended: `ip = request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for','').split(',')[0].strip() or request.client.host`.

minor.issue: JWT secret stored in .env wrapped in double quotes (`JWT_SECRET="9c4e..."`). os.environ reads it WITH the quotes, so the secret literally includes quote chars. Works because both encode+decode use the same string, but it's fragile and not portable.

Cookie uses `secure=True, samesite='none'` hardcoded. In local dev over plain http this cookie is rejected by the browser (works in this preview env because it's https). Frontend bearer-token fallback compensates.

issues: React Router v7 future-flag warnings still in console (cosmetic) — unchanged from iter1.

verified_flows.register: POST /api/auth/register sets cookie+returns token; UI redirects to /account
verified_flows.account_page: shows email, has logout-btn; sign out returns to /
verified_flows.product_detail_reviews: REVIEWS section present; write-review-btn shown when logged in
verified_flows.cart_full_page_checkout: /cart 'CHECKOUT' button → /checkout (verified by rendering, click via data-testid='checkout-btn')
verified_flows.checkout_page: email + first/last name + address1 + city + region(<select>) + zip + country(<select>) + phone fields present; order summary shows subtotal + $6.99 shipping + total; proceed-payment-btn present

action_items: HIGH: Fix brute-force lockout — use X-Forwarded-For (or email-only identifier) in /app/backend/auth.py login() so it works behind the ingress proxy.
LOW: Remove quotes around JWT_SECRET value in /app/backend/.env.
LOW: From iter1 still open — enable React Router v7 future flags to silence console warnings.
LOW: Memoise emergentintegrations.StripeCheckout instead of re-instantiating per request.
critical_code_review_comments: Lockout: identifier built from request.client.host is unreliable behind any reverse proxy — must use X-Forwarded-For. This is the only material defect found.

auth.py: JWT decode catches ExpiredSignatureError + InvalidTokenError but not bson.errors.InvalidId from ObjectId(payload['sub']) when sub is malformed. Would surface as 500 on a crafted token; very low risk because we always sign sub as a valid ObjectId hex.
server.py: /api/checkout/status now correctly returns {status:'not_found'} for bogus session_ids (iter1 action item — fixed by main agent ✅).
server.py: payment_transactions docs store created_at as isoformat string AND .reviews stores it as datetime — inconsistent, would complicate analytics later.
Reviews endpoint properly enforces verified-buyer via $or check on email+product_id; idempotent upsert per (product_id,user_id) works.
All new frontend interactive elements carry data-testid as specified — testability remains excellent.

abandoned_cart_worker runs every 5min and only emails carts ≥ ABANDONED_CART_DELAY_MIN (60) old — sensible but cannot be verified in a single test run without waiting 60min; logic reviewed and looks correct (it flips status to 'emailed' to prevent re-sends).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (rewritten — 26 tests covering all new endpoints + isolation fixes)
test_credentials: Created on the fly per test via POST /api/auth/register (qa-*@example.com / Qatest1234). /app/memory/test_credentials.md does NOT exist — flagged for main agent to create if needed. Seeded test1@example.com/testpass123 (mentioned in request) was not used.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert temporary users + payment_transactions + abandoned_carts and clean them up in class teardown. No persistent test data left over (verified login_attempts collection empty after run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 26/26 passing. ONE backend defect remains: brute-force lockout does not engage behind k8s ingress because request.client.host varies per pod (see action_items). Frontend already proven working in this run; CheckoutPage uses <select> for region & country (must use page.select_option, not fill). Verified-buyer review flow requires inserting a paid payment_transactions doc manually — pattern in TestReviews.test_verified_buyer_can_review_and_update.
rca_of_the_issue: Brute-force lockout: in /app/backend/auth.py login(), identifier = f'{request.client.host}:{email}'. Behind the kubernetes ingress, request.client.host is the upstream container's IP, not the originating client IP. Each TCP connection from the ingress to an upstream pod may resolve to a different pod IP, so the same logical user attacking with one IP creates multiple identifier rows in login_attempts, each with count < 5. Mitigation: parse the first IP from X-Forwarded-For header. Confirmation: pymongo query after 7 failed logins for one email returned two distinct rows '10.227.1.2:<email>' (count=5) and '10.227.1.6:<email>' (count=2).
